steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The grammatical subject is “Neuros Medical’s technology” — singular. The phrase “alongside other such neuromodulation technologies” is a parenthetical modifier set off by commas; it does not change the subject. The blank needs a complete verb that agrees with a singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — paving: a participle with no auxiliary verb; creates an incomplete verb phrase and a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice B — is paving: singular auxiliary ‘is’ with the present participle forms a complete verb that agrees with the singular subject ‘technology.’ ✓
  • Choice C — are paving: plural auxiliary; does not agree with the singular subject ‘technology.’ ✗
  • Choice D — have paved: plural auxiliary; does not agree with the singular subject ‘technology.’ ✗
That confirms B is correct.
